mnemonics.title: K-map Grouping Rules
mnemonics.content: GROUP: Always group in powers of 2 (1, 2, 4, 8, 16). OVERLAP: Cells can be in multiple groups. WRAP: Groups can wrap around edges. MAXIMIZE: Make groups as large as possible.

mnemonics.title: JK Flip-Flop Truth Table
mnemonics.content: J K Q+ - Remember: 'J ump to Set, K ill to Reset, boTh Toggle, No change for None' J=1,K=0 → Set; J=0,K=1 → Reset; J=1,K=1 → Toggle; J=0,K=0 → No change

mnemonics.title: D Flip-Flop Behavior
mnemonics.content: D is for 'Data' or 'Delay' - Output equals input at next clock. Q+ = D. Simple to remember: Whatever is at D, goes to Q (after clock).

mnemonics.title: T Flip-Flop Behavior
mnemonics.content: T is for 'Toggle' - T=0 → No change (Q+ = Q); T=1 → Toggle (Q+ = Q'). Remember: T flips when 1.

acronyms.usage: Graphical method for Boolean algebra simplification, named after Maurice Karnaugh.
Boolean expression form where terms are ANDed then ORed. Used with K-maps grouping 1s.
Boolean expression form where terms are ORed then ANDed. Used with K-maps grouping 0s.
Stores data; output equals input after one clock cycle. Most common in registers.
Basic flip-flop with Set and Reset inputs. Has forbidden state when both are 1.
Versatile flip-flop; universal type. Can be converted to any other flip-flop.
Toggles output when T=1. Used in counters and frequency dividers.
Configuration eliminating race-around condition in JK flip-flops.

visualTips.visualization: Think of K-map as a toroid (doughnut) where top wraps to bottom, left wraps to right. Each cell has exactly 3 neighbors in 3-variable, 4 neighbors in 4-variable map.
In K-map labels (00, 01, 11, 10), only ONE bit changes between adjacent cells. This ensures Gray code property essential for proper simplification.
Draw a state circle with Q (current) and show arrows to Q+ (next) labeled with input conditions. Helps visualize excitation table.
Each doubling of group size (1→2→4→8→16) eliminates ONE variable. Group of 2 eliminates 1 var, group of 4 eliminates 2 vars, etc.

analogies.analogy: Like compressing a photo - adjacent similar pixels (1s) are grouped to represent simplified information. Larger groups = more compression = simpler expression.
Think of flip-flop as a light switch with memory. Flipping (clock) decides when you can change position, and it stays in that position until next change.
Combinational is like a calculator (input → calculation → output). Sequential is like a person remembering previous calculations and using them for current task.
Like a relay system where master receives message during day (clock high), slave delivers it only at night (clock fall). Prevents message confusion.
Unlike level triggering (door open during entire time), edge triggering is like a photograph - captures exact moment (clock edge) only.
